title: eriogonum nudum subsp. saxicola
intro: Eriogonum nudum subsp. saxicola, also known as the naked buckwheat, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Eriogonum nudum subsp. saxicola include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to promote skin health and soothe injuries.
